prompt:
We run a small fabrication shop and I keep missing maintenance windows, so: a recurrence engine, driven entirely from the shell. Entry point `remind.py`, reminders live in a JSON file picked by `--file PATH`. One thing before anything else: NO wall clock. Every command takes explicit dates. We replay history in audits and in tests, and the last tool that called datetime.today() deep inside the scheduling math was unusable -- if I see now()/today() in this codebase the PR bounces.

Commands:
1. `add TITLE --rule daily|weekly|monthly --start YYYY-MM-DD [--interval N] [--count N | --until YYYY-MM-DD] [--byday MO,TU,WE,TH,FR,SA,SU]` -> prints `added <id>`; integer ids from 1, in order.
2. `skip ID YYYY-MM-DD` -> records an exception date for that reminder; silent on success; skipping the same date twice is fine.
3. `next ID --from YYYY-MM-DD [--n N]` -> the next N occurrences on-or-after --from, one YYYY-MM-DD per line; --n defaults to 5; print fewer (or nothing) if the rule runs out. Exit 0 either way.
4. `agenda --on YYYY-MM-DD` -> every reminder that fires that day, one `<id> <title>` line each, ascending id; nothing prints if the day is empty.

Recurrence semantics -- I care about these, get them exact:
- daily: --start, then every --interval days (interval defaults to 1 everywhere).
- weekly: weeks start on Monday. The week containing --start is week 0; a week is active when its index is a multiple of --interval; occurrences are the --byday weekdays inside active weeks, excluding anything before --start itself. --byday is required for weekly and forbidden for the other rules.
- monthly: the same day-of-month as --start, every --interval months. Months that don't have that day (the 31st in June, say) simply produce no occurrence -- and such a month must NOT eat into --count.
- --count N means the first N occurrences the rule generates; --until is inclusive; giving both is an error.
- skip removes occurrences AFTER generation, iCal EXDATE style: a skipped date still consumes --count. agenda and next must both honor skips.

Exit codes: 2 for bad invocations (unknown rule, malformed date, --count < 1, --interval < 1, count together with until, missing/forbidden/garbled --byday), 1 for an unknown reminder id, 0 otherwise. Errors print to stderr and must never corrupt the store -- a failed add leaves the file byte-identical. Read-only commands against a store file that doesn't exist yet treat it as empty and must not create it.

Keep the recurrence math in its own module away from storage and CLI wiring; I want to unit-test it someday. Acceptance contract: `python3 test_remind.py`, unmodified, must pass.
